root_cause: OCS had bounded memory and continuity but lacked deterministic semantic resolution over memory, continuity, cognition, replay-derived intent, and registry context.
runtime_model: OCS semantic resolution consumes explicit OCS memory, continuity, cognition, replay-derived intent, and domain registry context inputs.
The runtime validates replay visibility, artifact hashes, known OCS artifact types, known OCS statuses, memory-continuity hash linkage, and false authority flags.
The runtime resolves semantic references from memory, continuity, cognition, intent candidates, and registry context.
The runtime resolves domain identities, capability identities, and worker identities.
Continuity-aware reference linking binds semantic references to operation groups.
Ambiguity detection records multiple identities or unresolved references.
Clarification candidates are generated only as non-authority evidence.
The semantic hash is derived from source hashes, registry context, semantic resolutions, continuity links, ambiguity evidence, clarification candidates, authority flags, status, and failure state.
Replay reconstruction validates ordering, wrapper hashes, artifact hashes, returned continuity, and semantic hash continuity.
replay_impact: OCS now has append-only replay-visible semantic resolution evidence.
Replay reconstruction can prove which memory, continuity, cognition, intent, and registry evidence shaped semantic identity resolution.
Identical OCS memory and continuity history produces the same semantic hash.
The runtime does not mutate source replay.
The runtime does not invoke providers, workers, PPP, approval, governance mutation, implementation, or execution.
